flags: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 95 days dark with 62 days to close. The promised disco/demo from 23 Mar never happened and the buyer has not responded. The single highest-leverage move is for the deal owner to directly re-engage Melissa (email + LinkedIn + phone) this week to confirm if the evaluation is still active. If no reply within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive check-in with a senior Jackson Lewis stakeholder (CFO or CIO from account contact list). If still no engagement, disqualify by end of July and stop investing.

MULTI-THREAD IMMEDIATELY UPON RE-ENGAGEMENT: Single-threaded to Melissa (Sr. Manager, Procurement) with no visibility into the evaluation committee, EB, DM, or cross-functional stakeholders (finance, legal, IT) who must approve a CLM + AP automation decision. The 23 Mar next step promised committee formation but we never met them. Upon re-engagement, demand introductions to at least three committee members, map who controls budget (likely the CFO or finance executive), and identify a champion with power and access. A procurement-only thread in a $90k, multi-product, AI-enabled deal is a loss waiting to happen.

RUN COMPETITIVE DISCOVERY BEFORE DEMO: Four competitors (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) are in play but we have zero intelligence on who has demoed, how the buyer ranks them, or what criteria they are using. Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.

deal_risk.text: Close date at real risk — 49 days remain to 27 Aug 2026 with zero discovery, demo, EB access, or contracting scoping completed against a four-vendor field.

No path to budget approval — a $90,000 purchase spanning iContract, iSource, eProcurement, and Merlin needs C-level sign-off, but neither the CFO (Andrew Charwat) nor the CTO (Clifford Forrester) has ever been contacted on this opportunity.
Risk of losing by default — with four active competitors in evaluation and no Zycus demo delivered, there is no visibility into whether the buyer has already progressed with a rival.

win_position.text: Buyer replied at intro — Melissa Geddie engaged with Zycus following the 15 Jan 2026 outreach, establishing initial two-way contact.

No economic buyer engaged — the only contact on record, Melissa Geddie (Sr. Manager, Procurement, Evaluator), has no signing authority for a $90,000, four-product purchase (OpportunityContactRole, as of 9 Jul 2026).
Discovery and demo never completed — the deal has sat in Qualified since 15 Jan 2026 (~6 months) with only one further sync on 23 Mar 2026 and no recorded discovery or demo session (Avoma manifest).

Four active competitors with zero competitive intelligence — Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, and JAGGAER are all named on the Competitors field with no call evidence on buyer preference (Salesforce Competitors__c).

deal_momentum.text: Cooling for over three months — the last verified buyer activity was the 23 Mar 2026 sync (~108 days ago as of 9 Jul 2026), with no confirmed two-way contact since.
Rep re-engagement pending reply — the owner emailed Melissa Geddie on 1 Jul 2026 (~8 days ago) to gauge project priority; no reply is logged as of 9 Jul 2026.
No forward next step — Next_Step__c records only a status-check email, not a scheduled forward milestone, as of 9 Jul 2026.

customer_commitment.text: Promised evaluation committee never confirmed — Melissa Geddie was to confirm committee members and discovery/demo availability after the 15 Jan 2026 intro, and this has not materialized as of 9 Jul 2026.
Single-threaded — only one buyer contact role exists on the opportunity despite a much larger account contact list that includes the CFO and CTO.

Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.
